last_completed_round.direction_name: Retile to a 4-warp CTA so each K-slice carries more MMA work and more resident warps
last_completed_round.hypothesis: The fixed shape hits the tensor path exactly, so the low tensor active 23.25% is not tail overhead. The current 2-warp 32x48 CTA only allows about 22 resident warps per SM (45.63% active, 48 registers/thread, 8.704 KB shared/block) and does very little math between full-block sync points, which matches the 19.10% barrier stall and leaves Tensor Cores underfed. Moving to a shape such as a 4-warp CTA that increases MMA work per staged tile should improve latency hiding and amortize the per-iteration barrier cost.
